scenario.executive_briefing: A 50.0% closure of the Strait of Hormuz over 10 days creates a daily deficit of 1.1 mbpd. National Strategic Petroleum Reserves provide 4.8 days of remaining buffer against a 22.0% drop in refinery utilization.
cascading_impacts: Crude supply deficit of 1.1 mbpd directly constraining national refinery throughput.
Anticipated global Brent crude price spike of 8.75%, widening current account deficit.
Domestic fuel retail prices projected to surge by 5.69%.
Macroeconomic GDP drag estimated at 0.1% due to elevated industrial energy costs.
Increased freight tariffs and supply chain delays across domestic logistics networks.
recommended_policy_directives: Authorize immediate Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) drawdown of up to 1.2 mbpd.
Contract alternative spot crude cargoes from West African and Latin American producers.
Direct Indian refiners (IOCL, BPCL, HPCL) to maximize middle distillate output and rationalize non-essential exports.
Implement diplomatic and maritime security coordination to restore corridor navigation.

procurement.executive_summary: Optimal procurement plan for Jamnagar & IOCL Complex secures 1.1 mbpd out of 1.1 mbpd target deficit at a total daily cost of $89,104,782.61. Blended quality achieves an API gravity of 31.39 and sulfur content of 2.0%.
actionable_steps: Issue immediate spot purchasing orders for allocated crude grades totaling 1.1 mbpd.
Establish Letters of Credit (LCs) via state banking syndicates for international crude vendors.
Fixture Very Large Crude Carrier (VLCC) tonnage for Atlantic basin routes (Brazil, West Africa) to mitigate freight spikes.
Implement crude oil futures hedging on MCX/ICE to fix crack spread margins.
Notify refinery blend managers to adjust desulfurization units based on blended sulfur content of 2.0%.
risk_tradeoffs: Lead-time trade-off: Long-haul Atlantic crudes take up to 25-28 days transit versus 10-12 days for Persian Gulf routes.
Quality balance: Heavy/sweet grades blended to comply with refinery maximum sulfur limit of 2.0%.
Unfilled shortfall: 0.0 mbpd deficit remains unfulfilled due to market supply limits.
Freight price exposure: Elevated maritime risk premiums increase total landed cost per barrel.

spr.policy_briefing: Under scenario SIM-API-TEST-001, an estimated daily crude deficit of 1.10 mbpd over 10 days necessitates a cumulative SPR release of 11.00 million barrels. Ending SPR inventory stands at 24.00 mb (60.0% capacity) with national security alert level classified as AMBER.
inter_agency_actions: MoPNG: Issue emergency executive decree authorizing ISPRL to initiate phased crude discharge from strategic caverns.
ISPRL: Activate main cavern discharge pumps at Visakhapatnam, Mangalore, and Padur to deliver crude to coastal refinery terminals.
Ministry of Defense: Secure mandatory strategic crude floor buffer to ensure uninterrupted operational fuel supply for defense forces.
Indian Refiners (IOCL, BPCL, HPCL): Re-calibrate refinery distillation units to handle SPR crude slates and prioritize high-demand diesel production.
Ministry of External Affairs: Engage international energy forums (IEA / OPEC+) to coordinate global inventory releases and stabilize crude benchmarks.
spr.replenishment_strategy: Schedule post-crisis reserve replenishment to begin on Day 11 (post-stabilization). Execute phased spot purchases when Dated Brent crude drops below $75/bbl and secure long-term bilateral supply contracts with non-disrupted producers.
